events.title: Concrete Hours
events.blurb: Six hours of stripped-back industrial techno inside a decommissioned power plant.

article.lead: Six hours of stripped-back industrial techno inside a decommissioned power plant, with fog, steel and a Function-One rig that you feel before you hear.

sections.heading: The room
sections.text: Kraftwerk is a raw concrete hall the size of an aircraft hangar. There is almost no decoration — the building is the show. Sound bounces off bare walls, so the low end is enormous and the light rig does all the storytelling.

sections.heading: Know before you go
sections.text: Doors are strict and the queue moves slowly, so arrive before midnight. Cloakroom is cash only, phones are discouraged on the floor, and the best sound sits dead-centre under the main rig.

events.title: Golden Hour Rooftop
events.blurb: Sunset sets over the river with slow house and cold vermouth.

article.lead: Sunset sets over the river with slow, sun-warmed house and cold vermouth. The kind of early night that turns into a late one before you notice.

sections.heading: Why go early
sections.text: The magic is the light. Getting up to the roof an hour before sunset means you catch the sky turning over the Tejo while the first records are still gentle.

sections.heading: Bring a layer
sections.text: Lisbon rooftops cool fast once the sun drops and the wind comes off the water. A light jacket is the difference between staying for the peak and leaving early.

events.title: Deep End
events.blurb: Warm, vocal-led deep house in a canal-side warehouse.

article.lead: Warm, vocal-led deep house in a canal-side warehouse. A residents-only night — no headliner, no filler, just a room that reads the crowd.

sections.heading: Residents first
sections.text: There is no big name on the flyer on purpose. The DJs play here every month, they know the sound system, and they build a set over hours rather than chasing one big drop.

sections.heading: The setting
sections.text: An old canal warehouse with wooden beams and one long bar. Capacity is small, so it sells out quietly — grab a ticket in advance rather than hoping for the door.

events.title: Basement Cypher
events.blurb: Open-mic cyphers, boom-bap DJs and a live band that never plays the same set twice.

article.lead: Open-mic cyphers, boom-bap DJs and a live band that never plays the same set twice. Community first, ego at the door.

sections.heading: How the night runs
sections.text: The band opens, the DJ keeps it moving, and anyone can put their name down for the cypher. Newcomers are welcome — the crowd carries first-timers rather than judging them.

sections.heading: Get there for the band
sections.text: The live set at the top of the night is the best-kept secret. It is looser and warmer than the headline slot, and it sets the tone for everything after.

events.title: Late Jazz Room
events.blurb: A candlelit cellar, a trio that leans into the small hours, and a no-phones policy.

article.lead: A candlelit stone cellar, a trio that leans into the small hours, and a strict no-phones-on-the-floor policy that everyone quietly loves.

sections.heading: Listen, don't film
sections.text: Phones stay in pockets here, and the room is better for it. Without a wall of screens the music feels close, and the players respond to a crowd that is actually present.

sections.heading: Two sets a night
sections.text: There is an early set and a late set. The late one is where the trio stretches out and takes risks — worth staying up for if you can.

events.title: Analog Disco
events.blurb: All-vinyl disco and Italo cuts on a mirror-ball floor.

article.lead: All-vinyl disco and Italo cuts on a mirror-ball floor. Dress like it's 1979 and you'll fit right in.

sections.heading: Vinyl only
sections.text: Every record is played off wax, so expect the occasional pause between tracks — it's part of the charm. The selection digs deep into Italo, boogie and rare disco edits.

sections.heading: Dress the part
sections.text: This is the one night where effort is rewarded. The floor leans into the look, and a bit of sparkle goes a long way under that mirror ball.

events.title: Signal Lost
events.blurb: Address sent by text two hours before doors. Raw concrete, one strobe, serious sound.

article.lead: The address arrives by text two hours before doors. A raw concrete room, one strobe, and a very serious sound system carry the whole night.

sections.heading: How to get in
sections.text: Register on the night and the location drops to your phone late. It keeps the crowd small and switched-on, and it means no two editions happen in the same place twice.

sections.heading: What to expect
sections.text: Minimal decoration, maximal sound. This is a night for people who come to listen and move, not to be seen — leave the expectations of a big club at home.

events.title: First Chords
events.blurb: Three rising bands, one small stage, and a crowd close enough to read the setlist.

article.lead: Three rising bands, one small stage, and a crowd close enough to read the setlist. Real amps, real sweat, no barrier.

sections.heading: Catch them early
sections.text: Every band on this bill is on the way up. Seeing them now, in a room this size, is the story you tell in a few years when they're playing somewhere far bigger.

sections.heading: The room
sections.text: A refitted theatre with a low stage and a steep back, so sightlines are good from anywhere. Sound is dialled for guitars, not for a DJ — it hits differently.

events.title: Warehouse 12
events.blurb: A port-side warehouse cleared for one night of hypnotic, driving techno.

article.lead: A port-side warehouse cleared for one night of hypnotic, driving techno. Doors close at 1am — arrive early or miss it.

sections.heading: Hard doors
sections.text: The doors genuinely lock at 1am, no exceptions, to keep the room controlled for the people already inside. Plan your night around getting there before then.

sections.heading: The sound
sections.text: A single huge room by the docks, with the sound system aimed down the length of it. The result is a long, hypnotic build rather than a night of peaks and lulls.
